original_hypothesis.title: Manager Can Drain Reserved Withdrawal Assets via Arbitrary Call
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `manage` function allows the manager to execute arbitrary calls, including transferring the underlying token out of the vault. This allows a compromised manager to drain assets that are strictly reserved for already-fulfilled user withdrawals.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the manager is a trusted role intended to deploy capital to Panoptic pools, `reservedWithdrawalAssets` represents funds that have already been promised to users who are waiting to call `executeWithdrawal`. The `manage` function has no restrictions preventing the manager from transferring these reserved tokens. If the manager does so, users' `executeWithdrawal` calls will fail due to insufficient balance, permanently destroying their claims.

proof_of_signal.pattern: result = target.functionCallWithValue(data, value); with no restrictions

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Can violate core accounting invariants like reserved assets
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No post-call balance check ensuring balance >= reservedWithdrawalAssets

analysis_questions: Is the ability to drain reserved assets an accepted risk in the threat model?
Could a post-call balance check be added without breaking intended functionality?
validation_requirements: Call manage() to transfer all underlying tokens to an attacker address
Verify that executeWithdrawal reverts for users with pending claims

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Manager key is compromised. Attacker calls `manage(underlyingToken, transfer(attacker, balance), 0)`. All funds, including those reserved for users who successfully requested and had withdrawals fulfilled, are stolen. Users lose their shares (burned during request) and get no assets.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all vault funds, including reserved user funds

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests ensuring manage() cannot violate reserved assets invariant

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Large unauthorized token transfers from the vault

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
